description: Final corrective sprint to align the implemented backend with the TCC documentation and the original sprint specifications. Fixes 8 identified divergences without introducing new business features. Must be executed sequentially — each prompt depends on the previous ones.

context.dependencies: All previous sprints (1–11) must be complete
context.execution_order: Prompts MUST be executed in order. Each prompt assumes the previous ones succeeded.

prompts.name: extend-specialty-enum
prompts.description: Add PSYCHIATRY and DENTISTRY to the Specialty enum and update the triage prompt accordingly
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, read the existing files. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md. Check medicano_crew/docs/specs/feature-registry.md to understand what already exists.

Update apps/api/src/common/enums/specialty.enum.ts:
- Keep the existing /* istanbul ignore file */ comment if present
- The enum must contain ALL FIVE specialties referenced in the TCC abstract:
  export enum Specialty {
    MEDICINE = 'medicine',
    PSYCHOLOGY = 'psychology',
    PSYCHIATRY = 'psychiatry',
    DENTISTRY = 'dentistry',
    NUTRITION = 'nutrition',
  }

Update apps/api/src/chat/constants/triage-prompt.ts:
- The existing TRIAGE_SYSTEM_PROMPT lists only 3 specialties (medicine, psychology, nutrition)
- Update the bulleted list inside the prompt (item 3 of REGRAS OBRIGATÓRIAS) to mention all 5:
  - medicine (medicina geral / clínico geral)
  - psychology (psicologia)
  - psychiatry (psiquiatria)
  - dentistry (odontologia)
  - nutrition (nutrição)
- Keep all the rest of the prompt EXACTLY as it was — disclaimer, JSON output format, emergency rules, etc.

Do NOT change anything else.

prompts.name: rename-subscription-plans
prompts.description: Rename the SubscriptionPlan enum values to FREE/BASIC/PRO matching the TCC documentation, with limits 2/10/-1 (unlimited)

prompts.name: extend-user-schema
prompts.description: Add displayName and isActive fields to the User schema so AttendantsService can persist them

prompts.name: enrich-clinic-schema
prompts.description: Add CNPJ, structured address, specialties, description, scheduling config, linkedScheduling, and indexes to the Clinic schema

prompts.name: update-clinic-dtos
prompts.description: Update CreateClinicDto and UpdateClinicDto to validate the new schema fields

prompts.name: enrich-professional-schema
prompts.description: Add CPF, registration, structured address, name (required), description, autoConfirm, minCancelNoticeHours and indexes to Professional schema; change specialty type to Specialty enum

prompts.name: update-professional-dtos
prompts.description: Update CreateProfessionalDto and UpdateProfessionalDto to validate the new schema fields

prompts.name: linked-scheduling-conflict-logic
prompts.description: Update AppointmentsService.checkConflict to honor the clinic's linkedScheduling flag and add the PATCH endpoint

prompts.name: availability-date-range
prompts.description: Convert GetAvailabilityQueryDto and AvailabilityService.getAvailableSlots to accept fromDate + toDate range

prompts.name: enforce-clinic-professional-limit-on-assign
prompts.description: Inject SubscriptionsService into ClinicProfessionalsService and enforce the plan limit on professional assignment

prompts.name: update-shared-types
prompts.description: Update the @medicano/types package to mirror every schema change in this sprint

prompts.name: fix-existing-tests
prompts.description: Update existing test fixtures and mocks so the test suite passes after the schema changes
prompts.request:
Before making any changes, READ each test file and identify what needs updating because of the schema changes from the previous prompts. Follow all conventions defined in medicano_crew/docs/CONVENTIONS.md.

For EACH test file, perform the following corrections as applicable:

1. apps/api/src/subscriptions/tests/subscriptions.service.spec.ts:
   - Replace any reference to SubscriptionPlan.PROFESSIONAL or SubscriptionPlan.ENTERPRISE with .BASIC or .PRO respectively, depending on context.
   - Update any hardcoded plan strings ('professional', 'enterprise') to ('basic', 'pro').
   - Update enforceClinicProfessionalLimit tests to expect ForbiddenException (not BadRequestException) when the limit is reached.
   - Add a test case 'enforceClinicProfessionalLimit — no subscription defaults to FREE limits (2)': mock findByOwner to return null, currentCount=2, expect ForbiddenException; currentCount=1, expect resolves.
   - Add a test case 'enforceClinicProfessionalLimit — PRO plan never blocks': mock findByOwner to return { plan: 'pro' }, currentCount=999, expect resolves.

2. apps/api/src/clinics/tests/attendants.service.spec.ts:
   - Add a test case verifying that displayName and isActive are persisted: 'createAttendant — persists displayName and isActive=true'. Verify the user document created by mockUserModel.create includes those fields.

3. apps/api/src/appointments/tests/appointments.service.spec.ts:
   - The AppointmentsService now injects ClinicsService. Provide a mock in the TestingModule:
     const mockClinicsService = { findById: jest.fn().mockResolvedValue({ linkedScheduling: false }) };
     {
       provide: ClinicsService,
       useValue: mockClinicsService,
     }
   - Update existing tests where checkConflict is invoked to set up mockClinicsService.findById to return a clinic. Default: linkedScheduling=false (matches current behavior).
   - Add 3 new tests at the bottom of the describe block:
     a. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=true allows adjacent same-clinic appointments': mock clinic with linkedScheduling=true, existing appointment ends at 10:00, new appointment starts at 10:00 → no conflict.
     b. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=false blocks adjacent same-clinic appointments': mock clinic with linkedScheduling=false, same scenario → throws ConflictException.
     c. 'checkConflict — linkedScheduling=true still blocks strict overlap': mock clinic with linkedScheduling=true, new appointment 09:30-10:30 vs existing 09:00-10:00 → throws ConflictException.
   - In tests that exercise validateAvailability, replace any call expectation availabilityService.getAvailableSlots(professionalId, dateString) with availabilityService.getAvailableSlotsForDay(professionalId, dateString).

4. apps/api/src/availability/tests/availability.service.spec.ts:
   - The getAvailableSlots method now requires (professionalId, fromDate, toDate). Update test calls accordingly.
   - Add a new test 'getAvailableSlots — fromDate > toDate throws BadRequestException'.
   - Add a new test 'getAvailableSlots — range > 30 days throws BadRequestException'.
   - For tests that previously called the per-day version, switch them to call getAvailableSlotsForDay (the renamed method).

5. apps/api/src/availability/tests/schedule.service.spec.ts:
   - The ScheduleService now calls availabilityService.getAvailableSlots(id, fromDate, toDate). Update the mock expectation to verify both arguments.

6. apps/api/src/profile/tests/profile.service.spec.ts:
   - If any test creates a Patient document with a string address, update it to use a proper IAddress shape (the AddressDto fields).
   - If any test creates a Clinic or Professional fixture, ensure it now has cnpj/cpf, structured address, specialties (clinic), name (professional), registration (professional).

When in doubt about a fixture field, use these defaults:
- cnpj: '12345678000100'
- cpf: '12345678901'
- registration: 'CRM/SP 123456'
- specialties: [Specialty.MEDICINE]
- specialty: Specialty.MEDICINE
- address: { street: 'Rua Teste', number: '123', neighborhood: 'Centro', city: 'Campinas', state: 'SP', zipCode: '13000000' }

If a test was previously passing because of permissive Mongoose behavior with optional fields, and now fails because the schema requires them, the FIX is to add the missing fields to the fixture — do NOT change the schema back.

After making changes, run npx jest --listFailedTests mentally to spot remaining failures and address them. The DOD is: every test in the apps/api/src/**/*.spec.ts files passes.

prompts.name: verify-and-cleanup
prompts.description: Final verification: search for any remaining references to old names and clean up dead code
prompts.request:
This is a verification prompt. Do not write code unless cleanup is needed.

1. Run a grep across apps/api/src for the following obsolete identifiers and verify ZERO results remain (other than this comment):
   - 'SubscriptionPlan.PROFESSIONAL' (the enum value, not the role)
   - 'SubscriptionPlan.ENTERPRISE'
   - The string 'professional_limit' or 'professionalLimit' as a Subscription field
   - References to a plain string `address: string` on Clinic or Professional

2. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in apps/api and report any compilation errors. Fix them by adjusting imports, type assertions, or fixture data.

3. Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in packages/types and ensure it passes.

4. Verify that the Address subdocument is being used as an object literal (not a string) everywhere it appears in fixtures, DTOs, and tests.

5. Verify that Clinic.specialties (array) and Professional.specialty (single value) both reference the Specialty enum.

6. Verify the triage prompt mentions all 5 specialties.

7. Verify that ClinicsModule exports ClinicsService and SubscriptionsModule exports SubscriptionsService.

8. Final smoke check: open apps/api/src/app.module.ts and confirm every module that was relevant in this sprint is imported (no missing AppointmentsModule, AvailabilityModule, ClinicsModule, ProfessionalsModule, SubscriptionsModule, ProfileModule, etc.).

If any of these checks fail, fix the issue and verify again. Do not stop until the codebase compiles cleanly and all spec files match the new API contracts.

Report back the final status: PASSED or list of remaining issues.
